name: Dynamo-style quorum read/write
task: Create /tmp/eval_qkv/qkv.py with class QuorumKV(n, r, w). Constructor raises ValueError if r + w <= n (a valid quorum config needs r + w > n). Method write(key, value, timestamp, node, success_replicas) writes to the given replicas if len(success_replicas) >= w; per replica, last-write-wins ordered by the tuple (timestamp, node) — a larger timestamp wins, and on a timestamp tie the lexicographically larger `node` string wins. write returns True on successful quorum, False if len(success_replicas) < w (in which case nothing is written). read(key, response_replicas) returns the latest value (by the same (timestamp, node) ordering, across the responding replicas' stored copies) if len(response_replicas) >= r else None; it also performs read repair — propagate the winning (value, timestamp, node) to every replica in the response set whose stored (timestamp, node) is older or absent. Provide an inspection method replica_state(key, replica) returning the (value, timestamp, node) tuple currently stored for `key` at that replica index, or None if that replica holds nothing for the key.
